behavior_summary: When the player 'looks at' the shelf, the script emits a short descriptive text ('shelf ') and then yields/suspends the process back to the engine.
No branching, menus, flags, parameters, or local variables are used; behavior is a simple one-shot U8P_NSSHELF::look/description handler.
notable_control_flow: U8P_NSSHELF::look calls Bark('shelf ') to display the short description, immediately followed by a suspend operation that yields/terminates the U8P_NSSHELF::look process.
open_questions: Does the suspend call terminate the process permanently or only yield to the engine with potential resume semantics? (inferred as termination/yield; confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_NSSHELF::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown to the player when they examine the shelf.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine's U8P_NSSHELF::look/examine interaction for the shelf object (processType 0x0208).
Triggered when the player inspects or 'looks at' this shelf in-game.

function_map.name: U8P_NSSHELF::look
function_map.role: U8P_NSSHELF::look/description handler for the shelf object

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: yield/terminate the script process back to the engine

external_calls.purpose: Emit the player-facing short description text 'shelf '.
Yield or end the U8P_NSSHELF::look process after outputting the description (engine control handoff).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the player 'looks at' the shelf, the script emits a short descriptive text ('shelf ') and then yields/suspends the process back to the engine.
